input.topic: Ollama GGUF Model Loader Memory Disclosure Vulnerability (Bleeding Llama, CVE-2026-7482)

candidate_data.type: Heap out-of-bounds read / remote memory disclosure
candidate_data.platform: Ollama GGUF model loader and quantization path
candidate_data.known_exploitation: No public exploitation confirmed at intake; CISA ADP SSVC lists exploitation as none.

input.notes: Manual intake from a social-media security alert; corroborated with CVE.org/CVE AWG API, NVD/GitHub Advisory Database, Ollama PR #14406, fix commit 88d57d0, Ollama v0.17.1 release reference, and Cyera Research. CVE.org lists CVE-2026-7482 as PUBLISHED on 2026-05-04 by Echo. The CNA description says Ollama before 0.17.1 has a heap out-of-bounds read in the GGUF model loader: /api/create can accept an attacker-supplied GGUF file with tensor offset/size values exceeding file length; during quantization the server can read past the allocated heap buffer, and memory may be exfiltrated through a model artifact pushed via /api/push. Treat CVSS carefully: CVE.org lists CVSS v3.1 9.1 CRITICAL and CVSS v4.0 8.8 HIGH; GitHub Advisory currently displays high severity. Do not overstate exploitation: CISA ADP SSVC marks exploitation as none at intake, and no CISA KEV listing was found during this intake. Do not repeat inaccurate social-card wording that default Ollama internet-binds: the CVE/GHSA description says default deployments bind to 127.0.0.1, while documented OLLAMA_HOST=0.0.0.0 deployments are used and exposed in practice. Do not include the social graphic's DDoS/botnet narrative unless supported by primary sources; this task is about memory disclosure, sensitive data exposure, patching, and safe exposure controls.

history.note: Manual vulnerability intake for Bleeding Llama / CVE-2026-7482; social-card claims were corroborated and narrowed against CVE.org, GitHub Advisory, Ollama fix references, and Cyera Research.
Article generated, validated, and recorded against PR #608
